title: Static Pricing Configuration & Cost Calculator
goal: Provide a static pricing data file mapping model names to input/output cost per 1,000 tokens, and a cost calculation function that computes estimated cost from token counts using the defined formula.

in_scope: Define a static JSON pricing file (model_pricing.json) with pricing data for common Azure AI Foundry models
Create a cost calculation function implementing the formula: cost = (input_tokens/1000 × input_price) + (output_tokens/1000 × output_price)
Handle missing pricing data for unknown models — return 'N/A' or None
Support lookup by model name with case-insensitive matching
Load the pricing file once and cache it in memory
out_of_scope: Dynamic pricing retrieval from Azure APIs
Pricing for non-text modalities (image, audio, embedding)

inputs: Token counts from inference responses (provided at call time)
outputs: src/model_pricing.json — static pricing data file with model-to-cost mappings
src/pricing.py — module with load_pricing() and calculate_cost() functions
checklist: Populate src/model_pricing.json with pricing entries for common models: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4, gpt-35-turbo, phi-3, phi-3.5, phi-4
Each pricing entry must have keys: input_per_1k (float), output_per_1k (float)
Create src/pricing.py with a load_pricing() function that reads and parses model_pricing.json
Cache the parsed pricing data in a module-level variable to avoid repeated file I/O
Create a calculate_cost(model_name, input_tokens, output_tokens) function
Implement case-insensitive model name lookup in the pricing data
Apply the formula: cost = (input_tokens / 1000 * input_per_1k) + (output_tokens / 1000 * output_per_1k)
If model_name is not found in pricing data, return None to indicate 'N/A'
If input_tokens or output_tokens is None or missing, return None
acceptance_criteria: model_pricing.json is valid JSON and contains at least 5 model pricing entries
load_pricing() successfully parses the JSON file and returns a dict
calculate_cost('gpt-4o', 100, 50) returns the correct numeric result based on pricing data
The pricing file path is resolved relative to the module, not the working directory
review_focus: Pricing values are reasonable approximations of current Azure AI pricing (as of early 2026)
Case-insensitive matching handles model names with varying capitalization
File I/O only happens once; subsequent calls use cached data
Edge cases with zero tokens, None tokens, and missing models are handled
risks_or_notes: Pricing data becomes stale as Azure updates model pricing — document this limitation in the README
Model names in the pricing file must match the model_name field returned by deployment discovery (Part 03)
Consider supporting partial model name matching (e.g., 'gpt-4o' matches 'gpt-4o-2024-08-06') as a future enhancement
USD is assumed as the currency — no multi-currency support in MVP
definition_of_done: src/model_pricing.json contains valid pricing data for at least 5 models
src/pricing.py exists with load_pricing() and calculate_cost() functions
Cost calculation produces correct results for known models
Unknown models return None instead of raising errors
Functions are testable independently without Streamlit or SDK dependencies
